task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly calculates the trimmed mean, but could be more descriptive in handling an empty list.
trace: I used Python's built-in sort function to arrange the input list in ascending order. Then, I calculated the middle index of the list, considering edge cases where the list size is even. After that, I defined the indices for the minimum and maximum values (ignoring one occurrence each). Finally, I calculated the sum of the numbers at those indices, divided by the total number of elements minus 2 to find the trimmed mean. The function returns None if the input list is empty.
